Hey, what's going everyone? So six weeks, six pack, or six week abs challenge, it's up to you. I like six weeks, six pack just because it sounds cool, but it is totally up to you in terms of how you wanna do it. So there's obviously a lot of collateral that we've already put together for you, emails, the flyers, the text blasts, nutrition template. What we've essentially done from a food standpoint is that we actually swapped out all the food lists and adopted this for majority off of the calibration diet that Dr. Kashi puts new clients on. What that is, and mind you, like I'm a big anti-foo-foo person when it comes to nutrition, basically it's low, I think it's called low FODMAT foods, but basically foods that people have over general populations have the lowest reactivity to. So foods that are the least likely to cause allergens and inflammation, things like that. Like dairy is one that most people have at least a minor allergy to. People get gas, yawf of, you know, lots of grains a lot of times. Not that so much that they have an allergy, but they just aren't perfect digesters of it. And so what this does is it swaps out all the protein sources, all the carb sources, all the fat sources for foods that are basically zero on the likelihood that they cause an allergic reaction. So, anywho, by switching those things out, most people will lose a few pounds of water weight just in the beginning, even with identical calorie intake. And they usually start feeling better. And so you tie that together with, and mind you, where people are gonna be having the most, like loss is usually gonna be in their gut area anyways, because that's where the inflammation will be. But anyways, that being said, they're gonna be doing that in a deficit with foods that are gonna be less likely to cause swelling, which is gonna cause them to pee out extra water, but also with ab workouts that have been outlined. Pertice of Ms. Goosen, who's the first person to do this. Again, this is a zero fulfillment challenge. They're gonna be doing this at home. And then the actual calendar looks like this. And then every day, or every week, excuse me, you just go in and demonstrate what the workout would look like, proper execution, what to look out for. You put them in a group, post the workouts, and then they just follow the schedule.
